_comment: Realtek WLAN driver registry flags that override the OS PCIe ASPM setting. All must be zeroed for combo-card BT range fix (BT antenna shares with WLAN; if the WLAN goes into low-power the BT loses ~2-3m of range and drops audio). Keys live under HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Class\{4d36e972-e325-11ce-bfc1-08002be10318}\<0000|0001|...> — the network-adapter class GUID. Enumerate all 4-digit subkeys, read 'MatchingDeviceId' (or 'DriverDesc'), and only apply if it matches a Realtek WLAN chip from combo_cards.json (or if 'MatchingDeviceId' contains 'ven_10ec'). Source: session_2026-07-04_lenovo_slim7prox.md fix for RTL8822CE + BT range regression on Ryzen 6900HS.

flags.name: PciASPM
flags.description: PCIe Active State Power Management state override — the master switch. 0 = disabled.

flags.name: bLPS_PG_En
flags.description: Leisure Power Save + Power Gating enable.

flags.name: L1Support
flags.description: PCIe L1 substate support. Default 2 (L1.1 + L1.2). Safe to leave — only zero if L1 substate causes issues.

flags.name: L1OffSupport
flags.description: L1-off exit support. Default 1. Safe to leave.

notes.apply_after_write: Disable + re-enable the adapter (or reboot) for flags to take effect.
notes.revert: Snapshot the class subkey via reg export before writing; revert.ps1 restores it.
